Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Understand the Goal The objective is to analyze two POWL models of a Hire to Retire process identify the anomalies deviations from the standard normative process in each and decide which model is better closer to normative with a detailed justification

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Analyze the Standard Hire to Retire Process Normative flow Post Job Ad Screen Candidates Conduct Interviews Make Hiring Decision If hired Onboard Employee Add to Payroll Close Case Logical dependencies Must post before screening Must screen before interviewing usually Must interview before deciding Must decide before onboarding only hired candidates onboard Must onboard before adding to payroll Must add to payroll before closing the case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Analyze POWL Model 1 Nodes Post Screen Decide Interview Onboard Payroll Close Edges Post Screen Screen Decide Screen Interview Decide Onboard Onboard Payroll Payroll Close Implicit partial order relationships Post is strictly before Screen Screen is strictly before Decide AND Interview Since Screen Decide and Screen Interview Decide and Interview are not strictly ordered with respect to each other They can happen in parallel or in either order Interview then Decide or Decide then Interview

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Anomalies in Model 1 Lack of causal dependency between Interview and Decide A hiring decision logically requires interviews to have been conducted at least the final round By making them concurrent or allowing Decide to happen before Interview the process violates basic business logic You cannot decide to hire someone before interviewing them This is a severe anomaly

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Analyze POWL Model 2 Nodes Post Screen Interview Decide loop onboarding Onboard skip xor payroll Payroll skip Close Edges Post Screen Post Interview Interview Decide Decide loop onboarding loop onboarding xor payroll xor payroll Close Implicit partial order relationships Post Screen AND Post Interview Screen and Interview are unordered relative to each other Interview Decide Decide loop onboarding xor payroll Close

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Anomalies in Model 2 Screening without ordering relative to Interview Post Screen and Post Interview This implies a candidate can be interviewed before or concurrently with being screened Usually you screen resume application review before inviting them to interview This is a moderate anomaly Onboarding Loop The loop Onboard skip allows Onboarding to be repeated multiple times Onboarding is typically a one time event per hire While sometimes a checklist needs revisiting a general loop on the whole onboarding activity is a deviation from standard practice a minor moderate anomaly Payroll Optional The XOR Payroll skip allows the process to skip adding the employee to payroll A hired employee must be paid Skipping payroll is a severe administrative and legal violation This is a severe anomaly Missing causal link from Screen to Decide Interview properly Because Post Screen and Post Interview and Interview Decide Screen is completely disconnected from Decide and Interview logically You could screen candidates and just never do anything with the screened candidates while interviewing completely unscreened ones

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - IND
Text: Re evaluating severity In Model 1 the out of order Decide Interview is bad but perhaps one could argue in a strict graph interpretation it just says Screening must happen before both but they can happen in any order eg maybe you decide to reject before interviewing if screening was bad No Make Hiring Decision usually implies deciding who to hire among interviewed candidates But even if Decide means decide to interview the naming Make Hiring Decision implies post interview

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Conclusion on better model Model 1 is closer to the normative flow Even though it has the Decide Interview concurrency issue it enforces the sequential execution of Onboard Payroll Close without allowing them to be skipped In Model 2 the option to skip Payroll entirely breaks the fundamental integrity of hiring an employee Model 1s anomaly is an ordering constraint violation Model 2s anomaly is a missing mandatory step and a logical disconnect between screening and interviewing

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Self Correction during drafting Make sure to clearly state why Model 2 is worse Skipping Payroll is an absolute dealbreaker for an HR process Model 1s flaw is a precedence logic error Model 2s flaw is a structural integrity compliance error
